title: xyris linifolia
intro: Xyris linifolia, also known as the grass-leaved yellow-eyed grass, is a flowering plant species native to Australia, characterized by its distinctive yellow flowers and narrow, grass-like leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Xyris linifolia include its potential to aid in the treatment of skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and can also be made into a poultice or salve for topical application.
